message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker + Cargo.lock dependencies

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Updated timestamps and status on various tracked issues
- Reflects recent work across fcp-* crates

Cargo.lock:
- Updated dependency graph for new fcp-testkit/chrono dev-deps

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -107,15 +107,15 @@
 {"id":"bd-39j0","title":"[IDEA-29.1] Implement egress policy explain CLI","description":"## What
Implement `fcp net explain --url` and reusable evaluator API.

## How
- Parse URL; apply NetworkConstraints evaluation in dry-run mode.
- Output allow/deny + reason code + suggested allowlist changes.

## Success Criteria
- No network calls; purely local evaluation.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Evaluator performs no network I/O.
- Suggests minimal allowlist change when blocked.

Expose the lint via CLI checks (`fcp new --check`, `fcp manifest fix`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI outputs actionable guidance for fixing IDs.","status":"blocked","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:40:35.174567801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:07:07.812358453Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39sq","depends_on_id":"bd-1d8q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:40:35.348884404Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39sq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:42:40.762297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bus","title":"[IDEA-12] Structured Log Test Harness (fcp-testkit)","description":"# Structured Log Test Harness (fcp-testkit)

## Goal
Provide a reusable test harness that validates structured logs against the JSONL schema, enabling deterministic logging tests across crates.

## Background / Why
Testing/logging requirements demand JSONL logs, but tests currently validate logs ad hoc. A shared harness reduces duplication and enforces consistency.

## Scope
- Add log capture + schema validation helper in `fcp-testkit` or `fcp-conformance`.
- Provide examples for integration tests and E2E scripts.

## Deliverables
- `LogCapture` helper + `assert_logs_valid(schema)`.
- Example usage in one integration test.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the harness itself.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Logging validation can be enabled with a single helper call.","notes":"Extended fcp-testkit LogCapture to support test harness use: added push_line (manual JSONL line) and push_value (serde_json::Value -> JSONL) helpers; added unit tests validating schema success/failure and line numbering. fmt/check/clippy clean after changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"OliveDesert","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:39:12.376361299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:29:34.875837066Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bus","depends_on_id":"bd-11g2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:39:49.617428147Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3c0g","title":"[IDEA-25.1] Implement auto-tuning + presets","description":"## What
Implement parameter auto-tuning logic and preset table.

## How
- Evaluate MTU and path type.
- Select symbol size + repair factors from preset table.
- Expose config overrides.

## Success Criteria
- Default presets are applied deterministically based on path.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Selected parameters never exceed MTU safety limits.
- Overrides are validated and bounded.
","notes":"Added RaptorQPathProfile + RaptorQPreset, MTU-safe symbol sizing, preset->config, clamp helper, and tests for MTU/preset bounds in config.rs; re-exported in lib.rs. cargo fmt --check fails due to unrelated fcp-conformance formatting diffs; cargo check ok with existing warning; cargo clippy fails due to unrelated fcp-conformance lint errors.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedTower","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:46:58.284349412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T06:04:52.499712148Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3c0g","depends_on_id":"bd-f4sw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:46:58.291338948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ci5","title":"[IDEA-04.4] CursorStore tests + E2E scenario","description":"## Goal
Comprehensive tests for CursorStore behavior and resilience.

## Details
- Unit tests for schema encoding/decoding and monotonic constraints.
- E2E scenario with simulated node failover and lease transfer.

## Tests
- Deterministic JSONL logs (depends on bd-11g2).
- Failure injection (duplicate offsets, stale cursor writes).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CursorStore is safe against regressions and fork scenarios.
- E2E harness demonstrates safe resume.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:59.901308115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:08.480459622Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:08.480390884Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ci5","depends_on_id":"bd-11g2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:32:34.305999347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ci5","depends_on_id":"bd-2cqj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:59.916069942Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dl4","title":"[IDEA-21.2] Unit tests for zone policy diff","description":"## Coverage
- Capability add/remove diff.
- Transport policy changes (DERP/Funnel toggles).
- Ceiling changes (max risk tier).

## Logging
- Structured logs include policy ObjectIds.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).

 {"id":"bd-3drg","title":"[IDEA-08] Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping","description":"# Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping

## Goal
Standardize retryability classification and mapping to FCP error taxonomy across connectors.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram each implement custom retry classifiers. Consolidating into SDK avoids divergence and improves consistency (Retry-After, backoff with jitter, terminal errors).

## Scope
- SDK-level `RetryDecision` types.
- Helper to map external errors into FCP error codes + retry hints.

## Deliverables
- `RetryDecision` enum (Immediate, Backoff, After(Duration), Terminal).
- `RetryPolicy` helper that translates `RetryDecision` into backoff and logs.
- Error mapping guidance in docs.

## Tests
- Unit tests for mapping correctness.
- Integration test with simulated HTTP errors (429/5xx/parse errors).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can classify errors without ad-hoc string matching.
- Retry behavior is consistent across connectors.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:35:45.362878825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:11:48.319677117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:11:48.319586739Z","close_reason":"All child tasks (bd-1kt1, bd-qxnw, bd-33qn) completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3drg","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:21.618496408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3m2a","title":"TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)","description":"# TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)

## Goal
Add live integration tests for external services without mocks/fakes, using dedicated sandbox accounts.

## Scope
- Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram, Discord, Twitter connectors.
- Real API calls with rate limits and secret handling.

## Constraints
- Must run as manual-only or gated by secrets in CI.
- No production accounts, no recording or fake fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each connector has a live test plan and minimal smoke tests.
- Logs redact secrets and include correlation IDs and cost/latency metadata.

 {"id":"bd-3p99","title":"TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)","description":"# TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)

## Goal
Establish real-component unit/integration coverage (no mocks/fakes) plus a complete E2E script suite with rich structured logging.

## Definition: No mocks/fakes
- No mocking frameworks (wiremock), stub servers, or fake clients in the target suites.
- Tests exercise real code paths (actual protocol parsing, crypto, stores, mesh/session logic, sandbox enforcement).
- For external services (LLM, social APIs), tests must use dedicated sandbox accounts and real HTTP calls OR be marked manual-only with secrets gating.

## Deliverables
- Coverage inventory + gap map.
- Real-component test suites for core crates, mesh, store/repair, host+connector subprocess, sandbox/egress.
- E2E logging schema + validator + expanded scenario runner.

## Non-goals
- Do not delete existing mock-based tests; instead add real-component suites in parallel.
- No reliance on public production accounts.

## Success Criteria
- A coherent dependency graph of tasks that yields: (1) real-component unit/integration coverage, (2) comprehensive E2E scripts with machine-parseable logs.
","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"AzureHill","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:23:58.397206465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:00:28.519093518Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3pdj","title":"[IDEA-19] Structured Log Redaction + Secret Scanner","description":"## Background
Logging standards forbid secrets/PII, but enforcement is inconsistent. A mechanical scanner reduces risk and enforces compliance across tests and CI artifacts.

## Goals
- Provide a reusable log scanner that detects secrets/PII patterns in JSONL logs.
- Integrate with test harnesses and compliance runner.
- Allow explicit allowlists for safe test fixtures.

## Non-goals
- Replacing runtime redaction; this is a verification layer.

## Approach
- Add a `LogRedactionScanner` in `fcp-testkit` or `fcp-conformance`.
- Detect common secret formats (JWTs, API keys, auth headers, base64 blobs) and obvious PII.
- Expose CLI for scanning artifacts.

## Risks
- False positives; provide explicit per-test allowlists and explainable matches.

## Success Criteria
- CI fails if secrets appear in logs.
- Scanner provides actionable match locations and suggestions.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Scanner detects JWTs, bearer tokens, and API key patterns in JSONL.
- Allowlist requires explicit justification string.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:42:56.838059269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:34:44.343311553Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:34:44.343241223Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-kjji","title":"[IDEA-16] Spec-to-Beads Consistency Linter","description":"## Background
`docs/STANDARD_Requirements_Index.md` is the spec→beads mapping, but it is manual and can drift. We need a mechanical check that every NORMATIVE requirement has an owning bead and test coverage.

## Goals
- Parse the requirements index and verify every referenced bead ID exists.
- Detect missing owners/tests or stale bead references.
- Emit a machine-readable JSON report and fail CI on violations.

## Non-goals
- Auto-edit the docs or create beads automatically.
- Replace `br`/`bv` workflows.

## Approach
- Add a small CLI (prefer `fcp-conformance` or `fcp-cli` subcommand).
- Parse the markdown tables into structured entries.
- Validate bead IDs by reading `br list --json` output.
- Provide clear, actionable error output with file/section context.

## Risks
- Markdown formatting changes could break parsing → use a tolerant parser and add fixtures.

## Success Criteria
- CI fails when a requirements row references a missing bead.
- Report identifies missing owner/test entries with exact section names.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- CLI exits non‑zero when any referenced bead ID is missing.
- Report includes section title + row number for each failure.
- JSON output is stable across runs for identical input.

 {"id":"bd-m3p2","title":"[IDEA-03.2] SDK SequentialEventProcessor utility","description":"## Goal
Provide a reusable SDK helper for per-key sequential processing with bounded queues.

## Details
- Implement `SequentialEventProcessor` keyed by `stream_key`.
- Bounded queue per key + global cap to prevent unbounded memory.
- Configurable drop/timeout policy for slow consumers.

## Tests
- Unit tests for ordering within a key and fairness across keys.
- Stress test to ensure queue caps enforced.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can use the utility without custom queueing logic.
- Ordering within a key is preserved deterministically.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:42.619220006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:42.627441912Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m3p2","depends_on_id":"bd-xyyt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:42.627380959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mfu0","title":"[IDEA-02.2] RateLimitDeclarations: SDK helpers + runtime status","description":"## Goal
Expose rate limit declarations and runtime status in `fcp-sdk`.

## Details
- Add SDK helpers to map manifest `rate_limits` into `OperationInfo` / `Introspection`.
- Add `RateLimitStatus` reporting utilities and a standard error mapping for limit violations.
- Ensure retry-after hints are exposed for host/CLI.

## Tests
- Unit tests validating SDK mapping and error taxonomy.
- Integration tests with a test connector that simulates throttling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can advertise and report limits with minimal boilerplate.
- Rate limit violations are deterministic and include structured retry metadata.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:29:09.826725136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:04:51.365951495Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:04:51.365863842Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mfu0","depends_on_id":"bd-328m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:29:09.840938344Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-mfu0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation Complete - Created crates/fcp-sdk/src/ratelimit.rs with RateLimitError, RateLimitTracker (thread-safe pool tracking), and RateLimitPoolBuilder (fluent API).
